name: Crostini Di Polenta Ai Funghi (Polenta Crostini & Mushroo
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Fresh brown mushrooms, cleaned & sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the dried porcini mushrooms in warm water to cover for at least 45 minutes. Remove from liquid, rinse well under cold water, and pat dry.
2. Heat olive oil in a heavy saute pot and saute onion until translucent and soft. Add garlic and all the mushrooms. Reduce heat to low and cook, stirring intermittently, for 20-25 minutes until tender.
3. Add tomatoes, parsley, salt, and pepper; cook another 5 minutes.
4. To prepare polenta:
a. Bring salted water to vigorous boil in a large pot.
b. Reduce heat to low and slowly sprinkle cornmeal into the boiling water while whisking continuously.
c. Stir constantly until the polenta comes away from the side of the pot, after 20-25 minutes. Check for salt and let cool by pouring onto a baking sheet. Pat until smooth.
5. Cut polenta into slices that are 2-inch wide and 3-4 inches long. Brush lightly with olive oil.
6. Broil until firm and lightly crisp on both sides. Place a spoonful of hot mushroom sauce on top of each crostini and serve.
7. Can be served as an appetizer, antipasto, side dish, or main dish.
